decision_point: In an adult with a closed bony mallet finger involving less than one-third of the articular surface and no DIP subluxation, is extension splinting or surgical fixation preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with a closed bony mallet finger involving less than one-third of the articular surface and no DIP subluxation, is extension splinting or surgical fixation preferred?
Options:
  1. Extension splinting
  2. Surgical fixation
label: settled
label_rationale: Meta-analyses show no superiority of surgery; splinting is first-line, with surgery reserved for large fragments with volar subluxation or failed reduction.
